answer:
The interval E-B is a perfect fifth. To reduce it to a diminished fifth, you can lower the top note (B) by a half step or raise the bottom note (E) by a half step, giving two ways.  
Additionally, you could double-flat the top note or double-sharp the bottom note, but those are enharmonically equivalent to the first two methods.  
Thus, there are exactly 2 distinct ways to achieve a diminished fifth from E-B.

\boxed{A}
